briefs.headline: Markets Steady as Central Banks Signal Patience on Rate Cuts
briefs.lede: A quieter week ahead for global markets as policymakers opt for caution, while tensions in the South China Sea draw fresh diplomatic attention.

quote.text: We will not be rushed into decisions that the data does not yet support.

stories.title: Federal Reserve Holds Rates, Eyes Summer Window
stories.body: The Fed left its benchmark rate unchanged at 5.25–5.50%, with Chair Powell indicating the committee wants two to three more months of inflation data before considering cuts. Markets now price a 60% chance of a June reduction.

stories.title: South China Sea Tensions Rise After Naval Patrol Incident
stories.body: A US carrier group conducted freedom-of-navigation exercises near the Spratly Islands, drawing a sharp protest from Beijing. ASEAN foreign ministers called for de-escalation as shipping insurers raised premiums on routes through the strait.

stories.title: Apple Announces WWDC Date, AI Features Tipped
stories.body: Apple confirmed its annual developer conference for early June. Leaks suggest a major Siri overhaul powered by on-device models, and tighter integration with third-party AI tools — a direct response to pressure from Google and Microsoft.

stories.title: UK Budget Revision Pencils in £8bn Extra for NHS
stories.body: The Chancellor unveiled a mid-year spending update targeting NHS waiting lists, funded partly by a windfall levy on North Sea operators. Opposition critics called the measure 'too little, too late' ahead of a likely autumn election.
